response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has a long and varied software development career, but his background is overwhelmingly web-centric (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) and C++ is essentially absent from his professional identity — appearing only in a 6-month stint at National Instruments and a bachelor-thesis side project, and not listed among his skills at all. The role's core requirement is seasoned C++ engineering with STL mastery, multi-threaded systems, and industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443); none of these are evidenced. He has no background in industrial automation protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), no secure development lifecycle experience, and no industrial domain exposure beyond that brief NI role.
strengths: Solid distributed/event-driven architecture experience across multiple platforms and languages
Proven REST API development and high-throughput systems work (millions of requests/sec at Sociomantic)
Strong agile, TDD, and software-craftsmanship practices demonstrated consistently across career
concerns: C++ is effectively absent from the candidate's skill set and professional history — the role's primary language
No evidence of IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or any industrial cybersecurity knowledge
No experience with industrial automation protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850) or embedded/IoT systems at scale

key_factors.factor: Industrial domain and security knowledge gap
